mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack accelerates wage stagnation and suppresses consumer spending growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month, with a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 2017 baseline indicates weakening factory output and supply chain bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >50% of months where INDPRO falls below 100 during manufacturing contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 240, rising inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC after 2018.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of months with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent while real personal consumption expenditures stagnate, weak aggregate demand suppresses earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and consumption weakness signal fails to predict negative returns, with forward returns positive in >60% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside expanding housing starts reflects broad-based capacity utilization and investment demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The supply-side expansion signal generates negative forward returns in >55% of early-cycle recovery periods, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 4-year sample.
